name: Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora - circular from Serón
name_original: Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora — circular desde Serón
region_intro: Touring cycling around Serón offers diverse terrain on the northern slopes of the Sierra de los Filabres, overlooking the Almanzora Valley. The region features varied elevation, from valley roads to mountain ascents, providing panoramic views across the landscape. This area is characterized by its natural beauty and preserves a significant industrial heritage, particularly along its greenways. The landscape includes paths showcasing indigenous flora and architectural features. Best touring cycling routes around Serón The most popular touring cycling route is Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora - circular from Serón, a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a historic greenway, highlighting the region's industrial past. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Old Tíjola train Station – Serón Railway Station loop from Estación de Serón, a difficult 75.5 miles (121.4 km) path. This route features significant mountain ascents, offering extensive panoramic views across the landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Serón Railway Station – Calar Alto (2,168 m) loop from Estación de Serón, a 34.3 miles (55.3 km) trail leading through the Sierra de los Filabres towards the Calar Alto Observatory, often completed in about 4 hours 18 minutes. Touring cycling around Serón is defined by the Sierra de los Filabres, the Almanzora Valley, and routes showcasing industrial heritage. The network offers options for both moderate rides and demanding climbs. The routes in Serón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Serón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Serón?
A: Serón, situated on the northern slopes of the Sierra de los Filabres and overlooking the Almanzora Valley, offers a diverse cycling landscape. You'll encounter varied terrain, from valley roads suitable for more moderate rides to challenging mountain ascents with significant elevation gains. The region is known for its picturesque paths that showcase indigenous flora and fauna, as well as architectural features, including remnants of its industrial past along greenways.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate touring cycling routes suitable for families in Serón?
A: Yes, Serón offers moderate routes that can be suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055014113/via-verde-del-valle-del-almanzora-circular-from-seron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora - circular from Serón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 15.0 km trail with 220m of elevation, following a historic greenway. This route is generally less demanding and provides a pleasant experience through the region's industrial heritage, making it a good option for those seeking a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some of the more challenging touring cycling routes in the Serón area?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Serón provides several difficult routes with substantial elevation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Velefique Pass – Serón Railway Station loop from Estación de Serón&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 121.4 km path featuring over 3,200m of ascent, offering extensive panoramic views. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20119106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serón Railway Station – Calar Alto (2,168 m) loop from Estación de Serón&lt;/a&gt;, a 55.3 km route with over 1,500m of climbing, leading to the notable summit of Calar Alto.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes in Serón?
A: Cycling routes around Serón offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Baza and Los Filabres&lt;/a&gt;. You can also discover viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4625289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Piedra Lobera&lt;/a&gt;, natural monuments such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2547534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Perica Spring&lt;/a&gt;, and even a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2650515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Huevo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The region's industrial heritage, including the old mining town of Las Menas, is also a significant attraction along some paths.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available in Serón?
A: Yes, Serón features several circular touring cycling routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055014113/via-verde-del-valle-del-almanzora-circular-from-seron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora - circular from Serón&lt;/a&gt;. For more challenging circular rides, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Velefique Pass – Serón Railway Station loop from Estación de Serón&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20118876" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serón Railway Station – Serón Train Station loop from Serón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Serón?
A: The region's climate generally makes it suitable for cycling for much of the year. However, given its location in the Sierra de los Filabres, spring and autumn often provide the most comfortable temperatures for touring cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and the colder conditions at higher elevations in winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Can I find routes that highlight Serón's industrial heritage?
A: Absolutely. Serón has a rich industrial past, particularly related to ferrous deposits. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055014113/via-verde-del-valle-del-almanzora-circular-from-seron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora - circular from Serón&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, following a historic greenway that showcases this heritage. The old mining town of Las Menas is also part of this historical landscape, offering routes where you can learn about the municipality's past.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by the Nasrid Castle in Serón?
A: While specific touring cycling routes might not go directly through the narrow streets leading to the Nasrid Castle, many routes in the area offer views of Serón and its elevated position. The castle itself, a 13th-century defensive fortress, provides spectacular panoramic views of the Almanzora Valley and surrounding sierras, making it a worthwhile stop to visit on foot during your cycling trip.

Q: What amenities are available for cyclists in Serón, such as cafes or accommodation?
A: Serón is known for its gastronomy, particularly its hams and sausages, offering opportunities for culinary stops. While specific cafes directly on trails aren't detailed, the town of Serón itself and other villages along the routes will provide places to eat and drink. For accommodation, the region caters to visitors, and you can find options in Serón or nearby towns to suit cyclists' needs.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Serón?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes available around Serón, catering to various skill levels. These include 4 moderate routes and 11 difficult routes, ensuring options for both casual riders and those seeking a significant challenge.

Q: What is the average rating for touring cycling routes in Serón?
A: The touring cycling routes in Serón are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.75 out of 5 stars from over 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the panoramic views, and the opportunity to explore the region's natural beauty and industrial heritage.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Calar Alto summit?
A: Yes, for those looking to conquer significant climbs and enjoy high-altitude views, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20119106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serón Railway Station – Calar Alto (2,168 m) loop from Estación de Serón&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that leads directly to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6585569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calar Alto (2,168 m)&lt;/a&gt; summit, offering expansive vistas of the surrounding landscape.
tips: It is a beautiful station that has been maintained and rehabilitated to now serve visitors and travelers who arrive via the Greenway. I recommend you take advantage of the visit to recharge your batteries in its restaurant.
This is a particularly beautiful and well-kept part of the greenway that corresponds to the so-called Tramo del Hierro.

waypoints.name: Iron Section of the Serón Greenway
